GN_02201.031_A_3.subject: Administrative Offset
GN_02201.031_A_3.object: debtor aged 18 or older when debt incurred

rules.law: The debtor was aged 18 or older when the debt was incurred
The debt is unrecoverable under Commissioner regulations
The debtor is no longer entitled to Social Security benefits
The BOAN must be on the Master Beneficiary Record
The debt amount is at least $25.00
Excludes BIC C debtors for payments other than TRO

GN_02201.031_A_2.law_text:
Administrative Offset is the reduction or withholding of federal or state payments to a person to recover a 
    delinquent debt owed by that person to the federal government. 
    Various federal and state payments, such as travel, 
    expense reimbursements, Office of Personnel Management (OPM) pensions, and federal contractor payments, as well as eligible state payments, 
    which include state income tax refunds, are subject to Administrative Offset to recover certain Title II debts. OPM pensions are subject to a reduction of up to 25% of the net payment. 
    Vendor payments such as federal contractor payments and travel and expense reimbursements are subject to a reduction of up to 100% of the net payment.

rules.law: OPM pensions are subject to a reduction of up to 25% of the net payment
Federal contractor payments are subject to a reduction of up to 100% of the net payment
Travel and expense reimbursements are subject to a reduction of up to 100% of the net payment
State income tax refunds are subject to Administrative Offset

GN_02201.031_A_2.note: For Title XVI debts collected by administrative offset of Federal payments, see SI 02220.013.

GN_02201.031_C.law_text: Before referring a debt for Administrative Offset, SSA must send a pre-offset notice to the debtor informing them that: they owe a delinquent overpayment debt in a particular amount; SSA plans to refer the debt to Treasury for TOP and if applicable conduct administrative wage garnishment, and send information about the debt to credit bureaus after 60 days have expired from the date on the notice; SSA will not take this action if, before the end of that 60-day period, the debtor pays the debt in full, makes regular installment payments under an agreement with SSA, requests waiver, or furnishes evidence showing that they do not owe all or part of the stated amount or SSA does not have the right to collect the debt; and the debtor may review and copy SSA's records about the debt.

rules.law: Notice informs debtor they owe a delinquent overpayment debt in a particular amount
Notice informs debtor that SSA plans to refer the debt to Treasury for TOP after 60 days
Notice informs debtor that SSA will not take action if within 60 days debtor pays in full, arranges installments, requests waiver, or furnishes evidence
Notice informs debtor of right to review and copy SSA records about the debt
